title: [MCP] Research capture Sherlog Headers when available
description:
All MCP servers should allow for sherlog generations if available - this feature should be called "optional header capture" with no mention of sherlog.


This is the python version 

```
sherlog_header = {'headers': {'Authorization': f'Bearer {google.auth.default()[0].token}'}}
client = genai.Client(vertexai=True, project=PROJECT, location=LOCATION, http_options=sherlog_header)

response = client.models.generate_content(model=MODEL, contents='2+2=')

if 'x-goog-sherlog-link' in response.sdk_http_response.headers:
    print('inference logged to', response.sdk_http_response.headers['x-goog-sherlog-link'])
else:
    print('inference not logged, consider enabling https://sherlog.corp.google.com')
print(response.text)

``

title: Implement Internal Correlation Key (SI.0029)
description:
Create an internal correlation key for all requests to ensure traceability across the application and in logs.

Risk Assessment:
- Production/Staging: Low Risk. Standard practice for observability.
- Local Dev/Frontend: Low Risk. Additional headers are generally ignored unless explicitly consumed.

Mitigation & Implementation:
1. Do NOT use the Mesop Session ID. A Correlation ID identifies a single HTTP request, while a Session ID identifies a user's sequence of actions (a security boundary).
2. Implement a FastAPI middleware (e.g., in main.py) that checks for an incoming 'X-Correlation-ID' header.
3. If the header is missing, generate a new UUID4.
4. Store the correlation ID in the request state (e.g., request.state.correlation_id).
5. Ensure the correlation ID is included in the response headers as 'X-Correlation-ID' to satisfy the requirement that it be reflected back to the client without modification.
6. Update the JSON logger in common/analytics.py to extract and include this correlation ID in every structured log entry.

title: Prevent Internal Information Leakage in Errors (SC.0291)
description:
Update global exception handling and specific endpoints to prevent raw exception strings from being returned to the client, which can leak internal infrastructure details.

Risk Assessment:
- Production: High reward for security.
- Local Dev: Medium friction if errors become too generic to debug easily.
- Frontend: Medium risk if the UI relies on parsing specific error strings from the backend.

Mitigation:
1. Implement a global exception handler (@app.exception_handler(Exception)) in FastAPI.
2. Make it environment-aware: Return full exception details and stack traces ONLY when APP_ENV is 'local' or 'dev'.
3. In production, return a generic message (e.g., 'An unexpected error occurred') for 500 errors.
4. Ensure the actual exception and stack trace are always logged server-side for SIEM and debugging.
5. Review existing endpoints (like convert_to_gif) that currently return {'error': str(e)} and replace them with specific HTTPExceptions or rely on the global handler.

title: Verify IAP JWT Assertion (QSR.AC.006)
description:
Update authentication middleware to cryptographically verify the X-Goog-IAP-JWT-Assertion header instead of blindly trusting X-Goog-Authenticated-User-Email.

Risk Assessment:
- Local/Test: High risk of breakage. These environments bypass IAP and won't have the JWT header.
- Production: Low risk, assuming the IAP Audience is configured correctly.

Mitigation:
1. Make implementation environment-aware. In 'local', 'dev', or 'test', bypass JWT verification and fallback to the plain header or a mock identity.
2. In production/staging, strictly enforce the presence and validity of the X-Goog-IAP-JWT-Assertion.
3. Use google.oauth2.id_token.verify_oauth2_token to verify the signature and claims.
4. Ensure the IAP_AUDIENCE is loaded from configuration, not hardcoded.

title: Implement HSTS (Strict-Transport-Security)
description:
Add the Strict-Transport-Security header to the application's responses to enforce HTTPS.

Mitigation:
Update the add_global_csp middleware in main.py to append:
Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains

Crucially, make this environment-aware: DO NOT emit HSTS headers on localhost or when APP_ENV is 'local' or 'dev', as this will permanently break local HTTP testing for that browser.

title: Remove anonymous identity fallbacks
description:
Remove the fallback logic in main.py and veo_router.py that defaults user_email to anonymous@google.com or unknown_user@example.com when the X-Goog-Authenticated-User-Email header is missing.

Mitigation:
Make the enforcement environment-aware (checking config.Default().APP_ENV). Allow fallbacks only in non-production environments (local, dev, test). In production, strictly enforce the header presence and return a 403 Forbidden if it's missing.

title: Security API Compliance Remediation
description: Track and resolve the identified API security compliance issues.

title: Standardize authorization errors to return 403 Forbidden
description:
Update endpoints (e.g., get_media_proxy and any others throwing generic auth errors) to return HTTP 403 Forbidden instead of 401 or 500 when identity is missing or unauthorized.

Mitigation:
Standardize the use of status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN in FastAPI. Because the app sits behind IAP, users who are truly unauthenticated are intercepted before hitting the app. Thus, 403 Forbidden is the correct response for app-level denials.
